text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, semis torch everything. Semiconductors and mega-cap tech drove a high-conviction melt-up with SOXX +6.62% and QQQ +2.51% while SPY flatlined at +0.00%, showing a narrow but powerful leadership surge. U.S.–Iran de-escalation and the associated oil drawdown supported a pro‑growth bid, crushing energy (XLE -1.65%) and hard assets while reinforcing the Fed-friendly “lower oil is bullish” narrative. Positioning stayed hedged with SPY PCR_vol 1.17 and VIX 16.4 below its 20d MA 17.4, so the tape looked like upside pain for shorts and underweights in AI hardware rather than euphoric chase.

INDICES — VIX 16.4, drifting lower versus the 20d MA 17.4. Returns ranked: SOXX +6.62% > QQQ +2.51% > IWM +1.97% > DIA -0.15% > SPY +0.00%, with STYLE showing LARGE > SMALL by 0.54pp on the day. The standout cross-asset move was USO +0.56% over 1d against a -20.38% 20d slide, signaling a modest bounce inside a still-brutal crude downdraft. SPY and QQQ both remain above their 200-day moving averages, so no CTA sell trigger fired.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran 1.6x average and QQQ volume was implicitly above the 1.28x index breadth mean, confirming real participation behind a flat SPY and surging QQQ, which reads as rotation-driven accumulation into growth and away from cyclicals. Elevated tape in DIA 1.5x and IWM 1.4x versus softer SOXX 0.9x showed the index-level flows were broader than the headline chip strength, while sector volume skew (XLC 1.9x, XLU 1.9x vs XLK 0.8x) flagged some defensive nibbling and lower‑conviction in the tech blowout.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +3.04%, XLY Discretionary +1.45%, XLI Industrials +0.73%, XLU Utilities +0.67%, XLC Comms +0.23%, XLRE Real Estate -0.25%, XLB Materials -0.40%, XLP Staples -0.45%, XLV Healthcare -0.87%, XLF Financials -0.89%, XLE Energy -1.65%. Top industries were DRAM +9.66% and SOXX +6.62%, while the laggards were SLX -2.12% and OIH -2.93%, underscoring the tech–semis vs energy/metals split. Money flowed into AI‑linked hardware and growth (XLK, DRAM, SMH +5.76%, ARKG +5.19%) and came out of energy, metals, and traditional defensives (XLE, SLX, XLP -0.45%). The state is: AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.17, QQQ PCR_vol 1.47, and IWM PCR_vol 1.69 all signal strong demand for downside protection despite today’s risk-on tape. Positive skew in SPY +4.9%, QQQ +5.9%, and GLD +5.7% shows persistent put premium and dealer short‑gamma risk below GEX flips at SPY 752.0 and IWM 300.0, leaving room for amplified swings if spot backs up toward those levels. VIX 16.4, VXN 26.3, and RVX 22.9 all sit near or below their 20d MAs, indicating armor is on but not panicked.

CROSS-ASSET — The most notable chain was dollar strength with UUP +0.43% alongside rising Treasuries (TLT +0.49%, IEF +0.36%) and falling precious metals (GLD -0.38%, SLV -1.81%), a classic “real-yield up, duration and dollar bid, metals dumped” pattern. USO’s +0.56% 1d bounce against a -20.38% 20d collapse kept pressure on XLE -1.65% and OIH -2.93%, reinforcing the equity rotation away from energy into growth rather than signaling any real commodity turn.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 58.1/100 prints neutral, while the biggest reported single-name movers were KLAC +8.73% (The Fab 7), TER +7.19% (The Fab 7), and MRVL +7.27% (The Semi 7), with XLK the best 20d sector at +7.19% and XLE the worst at -10.08%. Vol spikes with price up in KLAC 7.1x, TER 4.9x, and MRVL 4.2x look like aggressive AI‑hardware accumulation, while GD’s 4.0x with -3.53% speaks to distribution in defense amid U.S.–Iran de-escalation headlines. PFE’s 3.6x with -2.74% underperforming an already weak XLV -0.87% flags idiosyncratic selling pressure, possibly tied to pipeline or pricing concerns. Watch INTC (price context from prior day range $121.40–$135.48 with current $133.98) for continued AI‑CPU sympathy momentum, GD -3.53% for potential mean-reversion if geopolitical risk re-prices, and EWP 5.9x volume with 0.00% price change for a latent Europe‑macro catalyst hiding beneath churn.
